itemListElement.reviewBody:
went for the interview, I quickly saw that this school isnt run by a principal it is run by a tyrant in a saree. Male teachers? Barely five, and only as window dressing for PGT posts. For women, the hidden rules are sick: if youre newly married, youd better be infertile; if you're young, you must never fall sick; and if you're older, your womb should already be retired. Because here, pregnancy is treated like a crime, and sick leave is a sin the principal will personally punish.


Shell even make you swear during the interview that you wont have children, as if she owns your life. Once you join, shell micromanage your every move, but God help you if you need a day off , she shall turn your workplace into a torture chamber. Want proof? Just ask the students how many teachers have run away mid-session. And if you're foolish enough to sign the contract, kiss your original marksheet goodbye. The principal keeps it hostage like a ransom note, and you'll only get it back after 3 to 4 years of until you get permanent.


The CHATGPTied reviews mentioned here are all by the teachers and principal herself. You can easily recognise their names when you come to know them.

This school lacks proper management, which is extremely poor and worse. The gap before PT1 is the maximum, yet students are given only one month to prepare for the half-yearly examinations. In just one month, teachers focus solely on completing their formalities and rush through half the book. As a result, students are suffering, but the administration seems indifferent and no action is taken, perhaps behaving like an illiterate.

One of the worst school in noida, the principal is an obnoxious lady who runs the school just for the sake of getting admission and collecting money. Each class has a strength of minimum of 35 students. The teachers keep on changing as they leave mostly after few months of service. My child has 4 different maths teacher in a single year .
Even the teachers talk about the inhumane environment. None of the teachers get a seat in the classroom with a minimum of 7 periods a day. 
The principal herself is seen shouting at teachers in front of the entire class.
They heep on conducting paid activities throughout the year and force you to participate and give money for it.
Even after you apply for TC,they fine you with different kind of penalties and refuse to provide you with transfer certificate.
My child's original documents such as birth certificate and immunization records were withheld.
One of the best school in Noida. Class room well maintained with air conditioning. Smart board. Big play ground with a big swimming pool.

I do not highly recommend Mayoor School, Noida. It does not excel in providing a well-rounded education. The dedicated and experienced faculty doesnt create a nurturing learning environment. The curriculum is not comprehensive and does not foster critical thinking. The school does not offer a wide range of extracurricular activities, discouraging students to explore their interests. I do not appreciate their commitment to individualized support, ensuring none of the students' success. The facilities are ancient and not well-maintained. It does not prepare students  academically or socially, instilling valuable life skills. It's a fantastic institution that prepares students for a disastrous future. I wholeheartedly endorse it to no parent seeking an excellent educational experience for their child. Btw I am from bihar
My son says that the school is low quality and teachers dont do anything worst school

Mayoor School in Noida is an excellent. Although my children did not enroll there, the staff exhibited remarkable hospitality and support. I must highlight Ms. Navneet, who proved to be exceptionally helpful and dedicated. Her hard work was evident as she attended calls even on holidays, weekends, and Sundays, always providing thorough answers to our inquiries.

Our experience with Mayoor School, Noida was disappointing. Despite enrolling our 3-year-old, we withdrew him mid-session due to concerning issues. Junior school teachers lacked adequate training in handling young children, displaying insensitivity and rudeness. Our child was traumatized when a teacher screamed at him and even threw his notebook on the ground. Despite confronting the teacher, the school took no substantial action. Similar negative experiences were shared by other parents, leading to a loss of confidence in the teaching staff. We strongly advise against selecting this school for junior school classes, as our unfortunate encounter raises serious concerns about the well-being of young students.

Worst school…..
5 years of experience with them taught me they just take your money and fill their pockets and decline the quality of the school and teachers
They also just let a child who hit someone else on the head with a compass continue in school because his father is a politician and they are scared. They don't care if someone else's child gets hurt. They just don't want to go against a politician.

I highly recommend Mayoor School, Noida! Both of my children have thrived under the direction of teachers who have taught here in Mayoor School, Noida for many years. This school has set a solid foundation for academics as well the school offer extracurricular activities which help the overall growth of the child. Levelled learning have enabled my children to gain confidence in their learning abilities. All thanks to Mayoor School
